text: Implementing strong security measures is essential in web development. One fundamental practice is using HTTPS, which encrypts data exchanged between the user's browser and the server, protecting sensitive information like passwords. Authentication systems, such as requiring strong passwords and using multi-factor authentication, help verify user identities. Equally important is input validation—checking all user-provided data to prevent malicious code or unexpected entries from harming the system. Developers must also be aware of common vulnerabilities like SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS), which attackers exploit to steal data or take control of websites. By focusing on these areas, developers can create safer, more reliable web applications.
